msg: Task 3.1 plan spec used plain path.resolve() to compare CLI-arg worktree paths against git worktree list --porcelain output, which prints realpaths; on macOS mktemp -d returns /var/... while git reports /private/var/..., causing spurious NOT_FOUND. Added a resolveRealOrAbs() helper (realpathSync with resolve fallback) and used it for the repoRoot-exclusion filter, --worktree-path matching, and the duplicate-path guard.

Plan Task 3.1 fixture spec implied exit 2 (invalid slug) for a 10000-char oversized slug hostile case, but SLUG_RE has no length bound so an all-lowercase 10000-char slug is valid syntax and correctly falls through to NOT_FOUND (exit 1). Adjusted fixture assertion to accept exit in {1,2} and confirmed no hang, matching the actual correct behavior rather than a hypothetical exit code.

cmdWorktreeReconcile uses resolveRealOrAbs() (Wave-3 helper) instead of plain path.resolve() for all worktree-path comparisons, since macOS mktemp -d paths under /tmp are realpath /private/tmp in git worktree list --porcelain output, which would otherwise misclassify in-sync worktrees as stale or as spurious adopt candidates in fixtures.

a1-reconcile fixture suite rewrites its own committed drift-*.md fixture timestamps in place on every run (test-side-effect pollution), unrelated to Wave 4 which only touched a1-worktree; consider mktemp-isolating reconcile fixtures per CONVENTIONS.md Isolation section

Task 5.1 plan assumed _test-fixtures/a1-pr-review/run-test.sh already followed the pass/fail-counter + last-line-summary convention (CONVENTIONS.md), but it used ad-hoc pass()/fail() echo helpers with set -euo pipefail and a plain All tests passed. line, which does not satisfy the plans own done-when (tail -1 | grep -q 0 failed). Converted the whole file to the standard pass=0 fail=0 counter/summary pattern, keeping all 8 pre-existing test bodies behavior-identical, before appending the new direct-path and hostile-input cases.

Big-block sed/awk cut for the io.cjs extraction (lines 270-841) accidentally removed PRODUCT_ROADMAP_KEY_ORDER/PRODUCT_FEATURE_KEY_ORDER, which the plan explicitly says must stay in the facade; caught immediately by the product-adopt fixture suite (ReferenceError), re-added the two consts right after the new lib/io.cjs require block, re-ran full gate green.

Wave 7 (extract lib/locks.cjs) verified PASS: 11 lock fns moved (0 in facade, defined in locks.cjs), done-when OK, ALL-SUITES-GREEN, product-docs stale-lock 68/0, facade -247 lines, no circular require, reservations.json not committed.

Mechanical move of product block to lib/product.cjs required also importing loadReservations from locks.cjs (used at 2 call sites in cmdProductStage/cmdProductAddFeature reservations handling) — not in the plan step 1 template destructure list but discovered by systematic free-identifier audit before wiring, not left for node --check to miss.

Task 9.1 line-count sub-check in its own done-when (wc -l < a1-tools.cjs < 6900) and plan SC-4 (shrink >= 2500 lines) both assume a 9294-line pre-split baseline. Real Wave-5-end baseline (before Wave 6 started) was 9584 lines (Waves 1-5 legitimately added ~290 lines: worktree adopt/reconcile, pr fallback, reservations --release, lock hardening). After all 4 mechanical moves (Waves 6-9, this task correctly moved exactly the product block 315-1815 = 1501 lines, net facade delta -1492 after the mandated pointer comment), facade is 7148 lines: 2436-line reduction (short of the 2500 target by 64) and 248 lines over the < 6900 numeric bar. All FUNCTIONAL done-when checks (syntax, runtime-load, facade smoke exit-code proof, zero remaining cmdProduct definitions) pass. No further reduction is available without either deleting plan-mandated pointer-documentation comments (Waves 6-8) to game the metric, or moving code outside this waves scope — both out of bounds for a mechanical-move task. Flagging for a1-victor-verifier / orchestrator: SC-4s numeric bar needs re-baselining against the real 9584 pre-split line count, not the plans stale 9294 assumption.

Wave 9 done-when FAILS: facade a1-tools.cjs is 7148 lines, done-when requires <6900 and SC-4 requires >=2500-line reduction; actual cumulative reduction is 2436 (real 9584 baseline) / 2146 (plan 9294 baseline) — below threshold. Product move itself is mechanically correct (all cmdProduct*/helpers gone from facade, runtime smoke passes).

SC-4 and Wave-9 done-when hard-coded a <6900 / >=2500 numeric bar against a 9294-line baseline, but Waves 1-5 legitimately grew the file to 9584 before the split — the numeric gate became unsatisfiable without moving code out of scope. Line-count acceptance bars should be relative to the immediate pre-split commit, not a stale absolute number captured before feature waves.
